tools.name: get_registry
tools.description: Returns the full attribute registry — every searchable and filterable field, its type, allowed values, and display label. Read this first to understand the schema before calling search_listings with attribute filters.

tools.name: search_listings
tools.description: Search commercial real estate listings. Returns paginated hits with facet counts. For AI-driven search, call interpret_search first to convert a natural-language query into structured filters, then pass those filters — and its bounds, when present — here.

tools.name: get_listing
tools.description: Retrieve the full details of a single listing by its hashid. Returns the complete ListingResource projection including parcel data, all attribute details, and attachments. Broker contact is included only when the requesting user is authorised.

tools.name: interpret_search
tools.description: Translate a natural-language property-search sentence into a structured filter payload compatible with search_listings. Use this as a transparent intermediate step: pass the user's raw query here, then forward the returned filters — and the returned bounds, when present (they carry the "near <place>

tools.name: save_search
tools.description: Save the current search as a named alert. The user will receive email digests when new matching listings appear. Requires authentication.

tools.name: list_my_saved_searches
tools.description: List the authenticated user's saved searches, including their filters and alert schedule. Requires authentication.

tools.name: delete_saved_search
tools.description: Delete a saved search by its ID. Only the owner may delete their own searches. Requires authentication.

tools.name: list_my_listings
tools.description: List all listings owned or brokered by the authenticated user. Optionally filter by status. Requires authentication.

tools.name: geocode_address
tools.description: Geocode a free-form US address into coordinates and structured components (street_address, city, county, state, postal_code) plus a formatted address and place_id — exactly the identity fields create_listing requires. Use this first, then pass the result straight into create_listing instead of suppl

tools.name: create_listing
tools.description: Create a new draft listing. Returns the full created listing exactly as stored, including its hashid and status. Pass an idempotency_key (any client-generated string, e.g. a UUID) to make the call safe to retry — re-sending the same key returns the original listing instead of creating a duplicate.

tools.name: update_listing
tools.description: Update a listing's parcel, pricing, or marketing data. Pass at least one group (parcel, pricing, marketing); send each group whole, not just the changed field. Returns the full updated listing exactly as stored.

tools.name: set_listing_attributes
tools.description: Set broker-authored attributes on a listing you own. Pass `attributes` as a map of registry field name to value (the field names get_registry returns, e.g. {"attr_parking_spaces": 40, "attr_office_class": "Class A"}). Only fields get_registry marks "writable":true can be set; publish-time computed f

tools.name: attach_listing_photo
tools.description: Attach a photo to a listing you own directly from its public URL — one call, no separate sign/upload/confirm. The server fetches the image and ingests it with auto-generated thumbnail/hero/full variants. Only https image URLs whose host is publicly routable are accepted. The photo is content-moderat

tools.name: sign_attachment_upload
tools.description: Begin an out-of-band file upload. Returns a presigned R2 PUT URL and the headers to use; the file bytes are uploaded directly to storage, never through this channel. After PUTting the file, call confirm_attachment_upload with the returned attachment_id. Supports listings (photos, documents), users (

tools.name: confirm_attachment_upload
tools.description: Finalize an out-of-band upload after the file bytes have been PUT to the presigned URL. Verifies the stored object matches what was signed (size and content-type) and marks the attachment ready. Returns the attachment snapshot with its public variant URLs. Idempotent: confirming an already-confirmed

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "get_registry" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Returns the full attribute registry — every searchable and filterable field, its type, allowed values, and display label. Read this first to understand the sche…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: save_search · Private data: get_listing, sign_attachment_upload, confirm_attachment_upload · Public sink: interpret_search, save_search, update_listing, set_listing_attributes, attach_listing_photo, sign_attachment_upload, confirm_attachment_upload · Destructive: delete_saved_search.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: save_search · Private data: get_listing, sign_attachment_upload, confirm_attachment_upload · Public sink: interpret_search, save_search, update_listing, set_listing_attributes, attach_listing_photo, sign_attachment_upload, confirm_attachment_upload · Destructive: delete_saved_search

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
